steps.title: Aromatics
steps.desc: Heat oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Sauté the garlic and shallots until fragrant and golden brown.

steps.title: Scramble Egg
steps.desc: Push the aromatics to the side of the pan. Pour in the beaten egg and scramble quickly until just set.

steps.title: Fry Rice
steps.desc: Add the cold cooked rice to the pan. Break up any clumps with a spatula and toss thoroughly with the egg and aromatics.

steps.title: Season
steps.desc: Drizzle the Kecap Manis over the rice. Season with a pinch of salt and white pepper. Stir-fry for 3-5 minutes until the rice is evenly coated and slightly caramelized.

steps.title: Final Garnish
steps.desc: Plate the rice and serve with sliced cucumber, tomato, and a fried egg on top if desired.

steps.title: Sauté Garlic
steps.desc: In a large pot, melt butter over medium heat. Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant but not browned.

steps.title: Simmer Liquids
steps.desc: Pour in the vegetable broth, heavy cream, and the dry pasta. Bring the mixture to a gentle boil.

steps.title: Cook Pasta
steps.desc: Reduce heat to a simmer. Cook for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent the pasta from sticking, until the liquid has thickened into a sauce.

steps.title: Finish with Cheese
steps.desc: Remove from heat. Stir in the Parmesan cheese until melted and the sauce is velvety. Season with black pepper and parsley.

steps.title: Prep Proteins
steps.desc: Fry the cubed tofu until the edges are golden and crispy. Set aside on a paper towel to drain excess oil.

steps.title: Blanch Veggies
steps.desc: Quickly blanch the bean sprouts and spinach in boiling water for 1 minute, then immediately shock in cold water to keep them crisp.

steps.title: Assemble Plate
steps.desc: Arrange the boiled potatoes, fried tofu, and blanched vegetables on a large platter or in individual bowls.

steps.title: Sauce Application
steps.desc: Warm the peanut sauce slightly and pour generously over the vegetables. Serve with crackers (krupuk) and a hard-boiled egg.

steps.title: Boil Lentils
steps.desc: Place rinsed lentils in a pot with 3 cups of water and turmeric. Bring to a boil, then simmer for 15 minutes until lentils are soft.

steps.title: Add Base
steps.desc: Stir in the crushed tomatoes and coconut milk. Let it simmer for another 5 minutes to allow flavors to meld.

steps.title: Wilt Greens
steps.desc: Fold in the fresh spinach and cook until just wilted. Season with salt and a squeeze of lime juice.

steps.title: Serve
steps.desc: Serve hot in a bowl, optionally topped with a dollop of yogurt or fresh cilantro alongside toasted bread.

steps.title: Marinate & Fry
steps.desc: Marinate chicken with salt and lime. Fry in oil until golden and fully cooked, then set aside.

steps.title: Sauté Aromatics
steps.desc: In a clean pan, melt the butter. Sauté the onions until they soften and become translucent.

steps.title: Make Sauce
steps.desc: Add Worcestershire sauce, a tablespoon of sweet soy sauce, and a pinch of sugar. Stir until the sauce bubbles and thickens.

steps.title: Toss Chicken
steps.desc: Return the fried chicken to the pan. Toss quickly so every piece is coated in the glossy butter sauce and serve immediately.

steps.title: Preheat
steps.desc: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a large baking sheet with parchment paper for easy cleanup.

steps.title: Seasoning
steps.desc: Place chicken and vegetables on the sheet. Drizzle with olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, salt, and pepper. Toss with hands to coat.

steps.title: Roast
steps.desc: Spread everything in a single layer. Bake for 20-25 minutes until the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 165°F.

steps.title: Rest & Serve
steps.desc: Let the chicken rest for 5 minutes before serving to keep it juicy. Serve as is or over a bed of rice.

steps.title: Fry Eggs
steps.desc: Deep fry or pan-fry the boiled eggs until a thin, golden, bubbly skin forms on the outside. Remove and drain.

steps.title: Sauté Sambal
steps.desc: In a pan with 2 tablespoons of oil, sauté the blended chilies, garlic, and chopped tomato until the oil starts to separate from the sauce.

steps.title: Seasoning
steps.desc: Add sugar and salt to the chili paste. The sugar helps balance the heat and gives the sauce a beautiful deep red color.

steps.title: Combine
steps.desc: Add the fried eggs into the chili sauce. Toss gently to coat and simmer for 2 minutes before serving.

steps.title: Warm Filling
steps.desc: In a skillet over medium heat, combine black beans, corn, and taco seasoning with a splash of water. Heat for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Toast Tortillas
steps.desc: In a separate dry pan, toast the tortillas for 30 seconds on each side until warm and slightly charred.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.desc: Fill each tortilla with the bean and corn mixture. Top with fresh avocado slices and a squeeze of lime.

steps.title: Optional Garnish
steps.desc: Add salsa or Greek yogurt (as a sour cream substitute) if available for extra flavor.

steps.title: Cook Chicken
steps.desc: Sauté chicken strips in a hot pan with oil until no longer pink. Add garlic and shallots for aroma.

steps.title: Vegetables
steps.desc: Toss in the shredded cabbage and any other greens. Stir-fry for 2 minutes until slightly softened but still crunchy.

steps.title: Mix Noodles
steps.desc: Add the pre-boiled noodles to the pan. Pour in the sweet soy sauce and oyster sauce.

steps.title: Final Sear
steps.desc: Stir-fry everything on high heat for 3 minutes to ensure the noodles are evenly coated and pick up a bit of smokiness from the pan.

steps.title: Marinate Beef
steps.desc: Toss the sliced beef with a tablespoon of soy sauce and cornstarch. Let it sit for 10 minutes while you prep veggies.

steps.title: Sear Beef
steps.desc: Heat oil in a wok until very hot. Sear the beef in batches until browned on both sides, then remove from the pan.

steps.title: Steam Broccoli
steps.desc: Add broccoli and a splash of water to the pan. Cover for 2 minutes to steam-cook the broccoli until tender-crisp.

steps.title: Thicken Sauce
steps.desc: Add ginger, the remaining soy sauce, and a pinch of sugar. Return beef to the pan and toss until the sauce thickens and coats everything.

steps.title: Cook Porridge
steps.desc: Combine rice and chicken stock in a large pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 30-40 minutes until creamy.

steps.title: Prep Toppings
steps.desc: While the porridge cooks, shred the boiled chicken and slice the cakwe and spring onions into thin rings.

steps.title: Adjust Consistency
steps.desc: If the porridge becomes too thick, add a splash more stock or water. Season with salt and white pepper to taste.

steps.title: Plating
steps.desc: Ladle the hot porridge into a bowl. Top with shredded chicken, cakwe, fried shallots, and a drizzle of sweet soy sauce.

steps.title: Vegetable Prep
steps.desc: Wash and dice the cucumber and tomatoes into bite-sized pieces. Place them in a large mixing bowl.

steps.title: Combine
steps.desc: Add the rinsed chickpeas and crumbled feta cheese to the bowl with the vegetables.

steps.title: Dress Salad
steps.desc: Pour the lemon vinaigrette (or a mix of olive oil and lemon juice) over the salad and toss gently to coat.

steps.title: Serve
steps.desc: Season with dried oregano and black pepper. Serve immediately or let it chill in the fridge for 30 minutes for better flavor.

steps.title: Make Broth
steps.desc: Boil chicken thighs in 5 cups of water with lemongrass, turmeric, and ginger. Simmer for 30 minutes until chicken is tender.

steps.title: Shred Chicken
steps.desc: Remove chicken from the broth, let it cool slightly, and shred the meat. Discard the bones.

steps.title: Season Soup
steps.desc: Taste the broth and season generously with salt, sugar, and white pepper. Keep the broth simmering on low.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.desc: Place a portion of vermicelli and cabbage in a bowl. Add the shredded chicken and pour the hot yellow broth over it.

steps.title: Garnish
steps.desc: Serve with a squeeze of lime, fresh celery, and a hard-boiled egg for an authentic Indonesian experience.

steps.title: Tuna Filling
steps.desc: In a small bowl, mix the drained tuna with mayo and sriracha until well combined. Adjust spice to your preference.

steps.title: Prep Hands
steps.desc: Wet your hands with water and a pinch of salt to prevent the rice from sticking to your skin.

steps.title: Shape Rice
steps.desc: Place a handful of warm rice in your palm. Make a small indentation in the center and add a teaspoon of the tuna mixture.

steps.title: Fold & Press
steps.desc: Cover the filling with more rice and press firmly into a triangle or ball shape. Ensure the filling is completely enclosed.

steps.title: Wrap
steps.desc: Wrap a strip of nori around the bottom of the rice ball. Repeat for the remaining rice and filling.

steps.title: Sauté Garlic
steps.desc: Heat oil in a pan and sauté minced garlic until golden. Add the meatballs and cook for 2 minutes.

steps.title: Hard Veggies
steps.desc: Add the carrots and a splash of water. Cook for 3 minutes until they start to soften.

steps.title: Green Veggies
steps.desc: Add the broccoli and cauliflower. Stir-fry for another 3 minutes on medium-high heat.

steps.title: Season & Thicken
steps.desc: Pour in the oyster sauce and salt. Stir in the cornstarch slurry and cook until the sauce thickens into a glossy glaze.

steps.title: Oven Prep
steps.desc: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Grease a large baking sheet or line it with foil.

steps.title: Toss Ingredients
steps.desc: Place the sliced sausages, peppers, and onions on the sheet. Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle with thyme, salt, and pepper.

steps.title: Roast
steps.desc: Bake for 25-30 minutes, tossing halfway through, until the sausages are browned and the vegetables are tender and slightly charred.

steps.title: Final Touch
steps.desc: Drizzle with a bit of balsamic vinegar or serve in toasted hoagie rolls for a complete meal.

steps.title: Make Starter
steps.desc: Mix wheat flour, garlic, salt, and 250ml water in a pot. Cook over low heat while stirring until it becomes a thick paste. Let it cool.

steps.title: Knead Dough
steps.desc: Add the egg to the paste and mix well. Gradually add tapioca flour while kneading gently until the dough is no longer sticky.

steps.title: Shape & Boil
steps.desc: Shape the dough into small logs or discs. Boil in water until they float to the surface, then drain.

steps.title: Fry
steps.desc: Once cooled, deep fry the boiled dough until the exterior is golden and crispy.

steps.title: Serve
steps.desc: Serve with 'Cuka' (spicy vinegar sauce), diced cucumber, and yellow noodles.

steps.title: Sauté Veggies
steps.desc: Sauté onions and mushrooms in butter until the mushrooms are browned and the onions are translucent.

steps.title: Toast Rice
steps.desc: Add the dry Arborio rice to the pan. Stir for 2 minutes until the edges of the grains become slightly translucent.

steps.title: Add Stock
steps.desc: Add warm stock one ladle at a time. Stir constantly and wait until the liquid is absorbed before adding the next ladle.

steps.title: Final Texture
steps.desc: Repeat until the rice is tender but firm (al dente). This usually takes about 18-20 minutes.

steps.title: Creamy Finish
steps.desc: Remove from heat. Stir in the Parmesan and a knob of butter to create a creamy, rich sauce.

steps.title: Mix Batter
steps.desc: In a bowl, combine corn kernels, flours, spring onion, turmeric, salt, and a splash of water to make a thick batter.

steps.title: Heat Oil
steps.desc: Heat about 1/2 inch of oil in a skillet over medium heat until a drop of batter sizzles immediately.

steps.title: Fry
steps.desc: Drop tablespoons of the batter into the hot oil, flattening them slightly with the back of the spoon.

steps.title: Golden Brown
steps.desc: Fry for 3-4 minutes per side until they are crispy and golden brown. Drain on paper towels and serve warm.

steps.title: Boil Pasta
steps.desc: Cook the penne in salted boiling water according to the package instructions. Reserve a small splash of pasta water.

steps.title: Cook Chicken
steps.desc: While pasta boils, sauté the chicken cubes in a pan with olive oil until fully cooked and slightly browned.

steps.title: Combine
steps.desc: Drain the pasta and return it to the pot. Add the cooked chicken, pesto sauce, and cherry tomatoes.

steps.title: Final Mix
steps.desc: Toss everything together over low heat. If the sauce is too thick, add the reserved pasta water to loosen it. Serve with parmesan.

steps.title: Parboil Beef
steps.desc: Boil beef cubes in water for 10 minutes to remove impurities. Drain and replace with 4 cups of fresh water, then simmer until tender.

steps.title: Sauté Spices
steps.desc: In a separate pan, sauté a paste of garlic, shallots, and ginger with lemongrass and bay leaves until the oil separates and smells fragrant.

steps.title: Combine
steps.desc: Add the sautéed spices into the beef pot. Pour in the coconut milk and evaporated milk while stirring constantly to prevent the milk from curdling.

steps.title: Simmer
steps.desc: Let the soup simmer on low heat for 10-15 minutes so the beef absorbs the creamy flavors. Season with salt, sugar, and white pepper.

steps.title: Serve
steps.desc: Garnish with fried shallots, sliced tomatoes, and lime juice. Serve with hot rice and crispy emping crackers.

steps.title: Make Glaze
steps.desc: In a small bowl, whisk together honey, soy sauce, lemon juice, and minced garlic.

steps.title: Sear Salmon
steps.desc: Heat oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Place salmon skin-side down and sear for 4 minutes until crispy. Flip and cook for 2 more minutes.

steps.title: Glaze & Veggies
steps.desc: Add the broccoli florets to the pan. Pour the honey mixture over the salmon and broccoli.

steps.title: Reduce Sauce
steps.desc: Simmer for 2-3 minutes until the sauce thickens into a sticky glaze and the broccoli is tender-crisp. Serve over steamed rice.

steps.title: Prepare Chicken
steps.desc: Use a pre-breaded frozen chicken cutlet or fry a fresh one until golden and extra crunchy. This 'crunch' is vital for the geprek texture.

steps.title: Grind Sambal
steps.desc: Using a mortar and pestle (cobek), grind raw chilies, garlic, salt, and sugar into a coarse paste.

steps.title: Sizzle
steps.desc: Carefully pour 2 tablespoons of smoking hot oil over the chili paste. It should sizzle and release a spicy aroma immediately.

steps.title: The Smash
steps.desc: Place the hot fried chicken on top of the sambal and smash it (geprek) with the pestle until the meat is shredded and coated in chili.

steps.title: Serve
steps.desc: Serve immediately with a mound of warm white rice and a few slices of fresh cucumber to cool the heat.

steps.title: Sauté Base
steps.desc: Heat oil in a skillet. Sauté onions and bell peppers for 5 minutes until soft and slightly browned.

steps.title: Simmer Sauce
steps.desc: Stir in the crushed tomatoes, cumin, paprika, and a pinch of chili flakes. Let the sauce simmer and thicken for 10 minutes.

steps.title: Poach Eggs
steps.desc: Use a spoon to make three small wells in the sauce. Gently crack an egg into each well.

steps.title: Cover & Cook
steps.desc: Cover the pan with a lid and cook for 5-8 minutes until the egg whites are set but the yolks are still runny.

steps.title: Garnish
steps.desc: Sprinkle with fresh parsley or feta cheese. Serve straight from the pan with crusty bread for dipping.

steps.title: Boil Water
steps.desc: Bring 6 cups of water to a boil in a large pot. Add tamarind paste, galangal, and bay leaves for the soup base.

steps.title: Cook Hard Veggies
steps.desc: Add the raw peanuts and corn rounds first, as they take the longest to cook. Boil for about 10 minutes.

steps.title: Add Soft Veggies
steps.desc: Stir in the chayote and long beans. Season with salt and a generous amount of sugar to get that classic sweet-and-sour balance.

steps.title: Final Simmer
steps.desc: Cook for another 10 minutes until all vegetables are tender. Serve warm or at room temperature alongside fried fish or tempeh.

steps.title: Brown Beef
steps.desc: Sauté ground beef with a little soy sauce and sesame oil until browned and fully cooked. Set aside.

steps.title: Veggies Prep
steps.desc: Briefly sauté the carrots and mushrooms separately with a pinch of salt. Blanch the spinach and squeeze out excess water.

steps.title: Bibimbap Sauce
steps.desc: Mix gochujang with a teaspoon of sugar, sesame oil, and a splash of water to create a smooth, pourable sauce.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.desc: Place a scoop of rice in a bowl. Arrange the beef and vegetables in neat piles on top. Place the fried egg in the center.

steps.title: Mix & Eat
steps.desc: Drizzle the sauce over everything. Mix thoroughly before eating so every grain of rice is coated in the spicy sauce.

steps.title: Layer the Pot
steps.desc: Arrange the tofu and tempeh slices in a wide pot so they don't overlap too much.

steps.title: Add Braising Liquid
steps.desc: Pour in the coconut water. Add palm sugar, coriander powder, garlic, and bay leaves. The liquid should almost cover the tofu.

steps.title: Slow Simmer
steps.desc: Cook on medium-low heat until the liquid has completely evaporated and the tofu/tempeh has turned a deep brown color.

steps.title: Quick Fry
steps.desc: Heat a small amount of oil and briefly fry the braised pieces for 1 minute per side to caramelize the sugars. Serve with chili sauce.

steps.title: Wilt Spinach
steps.desc: Melt butter in a small pan. Add spinach and cook for 1 minute until just wilted. Remove and set aside.

steps.title: Whisk Eggs
steps.desc: Beat the eggs with a pinch of salt and pepper until very bubbly.

steps.title: Cook Eggs
steps.desc: Pour eggs into the hot pan. Let them sit for 30 seconds, then gently lift the edges to let the raw egg flow underneath.

steps.title: Fill & Fold
steps.desc: When the top is almost set, add the spinach and feta to one half. Fold the other half over and cook for 30 more seconds.

steps.title: Sauté Paste
steps.desc: In a heavy-bottomed pot, sauté the rendang paste until it darkens and becomes very fragrant.

steps.title: Sear Beef
steps.desc: Add the beef cubes and cook until they are browned on all sides and coated in the paste.

steps.title: Simmer
steps.desc: Pour in the coconut milk and add the lime leaves. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to very low.

steps.title: Reduce Sauce
steps.desc: Simmer uncovered, stirring occasionally, until the liquid reduces and the oil starts to separate from the sauce (about 45 mins).

steps.title: The Finish
steps.desc: Stir in the toasted coconut (kerisik) to thicken and darken the sauce. Cook until the beef is tender and the sauce is thick and dark brown.

steps.title: Sauté Aromatics
steps.desc: Sauté chopped onion and garlic in oil until softened. Stir in the curry powder and cook for 30 seconds to toast the spices.

steps.title: Add Base
steps.desc: Add the chickpeas and coconut milk. If you like it saucier, add a splash of vegetable broth or water.

steps.title: Simmer
steps.desc: Let the curry bubble gently for 10 minutes so the chickpeas absorb the flavors. Season with salt and a squeeze of lime.

steps.title: Serve
steps.desc: Serve over rice or with toasted flatbread. Garnish generously with fresh cilantro.

steps.title: Fry Potatoes
steps.desc: Fry the cubed potatoes in oil until soft and golden. This method prevents the perkedel from being too watery compared to boiling.

steps.title: Mash
steps.desc: While still hot, mash the fried potatoes in a bowl until smooth. Add salt, pepper, nutmeg, celery, and fried shallots.

steps.title: Shape
steps.desc: Take a tablespoon of the mixture and shape it into a smooth, slightly flattened ball. Repeat until all mixture is used.

steps.title: Coating
steps.desc: Dip each potato ball into the beaten egg, ensuring it is fully coated. This creates the signature golden 'lace' when fried.

steps.title: Final Fry
steps.desc: Fry the patties in hot oil over medium heat until the egg coating is crispy and deep golden brown. Serve as a side dish.

steps.title: Melt Butter
steps.desc: In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium-high heat until it starts to bubble.

steps.title: Sauté Garlic
steps.desc: Add minced garlic and chili flakes. Cook for 1 minute until the garlic is fragrant but not burnt.

steps.title: Sear Shrimp
steps.desc: Add the shrimp to the pan in a single layer. Cook for 2 minutes per side until they turn pink and opaque.

steps.title: Deglaze
steps.desc: Stir in the lemon juice and a pinch of salt. Toss everything together to coat the shrimp in the garlic butter sauce and serve hot.

steps.title: Prepare Greens
steps.desc: Wash the kangkung thoroughly. Separate the tougher stems from the tender leaves and cut into 3-inch lengths.

steps.title: Toast Paste
steps.desc: Sauté garlic and shrimp paste in oil until the paste is fragrant and broken down. Add the sliced chilies.

steps.title: Stir-Fry
steps.desc: Add the stems first and stir-fry for 1 minute. Then add the leaves and oyster sauce. Cook on high heat for 2 more minutes.

steps.title: Season
steps.desc: Add a splash of water if it's too dry. Season with salt and sugar to taste, then serve immediately while the greens are vibrant.

steps.title: Make Sauce
steps.desc: Mix soy sauce, honey, and grated ginger in a small bowl until the honey is fully dissolved.

steps.title: Cook Chicken
steps.desc: Sauté chicken cubes in a pan over medium heat until they are golden brown and fully cooked through.

steps.title: Glaze
steps.desc: Pour the sauce over the chicken. Simmer for 3-5 minutes until the sauce reduces into a thick, shiny glaze.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.desc: Serve the glazed chicken over a bowl of steamed white rice. Add the steamed broccoli on the side for a balanced meal.

steps.title: Mix Meatballs
steps.desc: Combine ground chicken, tapioca flour, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Knead until the mixture is sticky and well-blended.

steps.title: Shape & Boil
steps.desc: Bring a pot of water to a simmer. Shape the mixture into small balls and drop them into the water. Remove once they float.

steps.title: Prepare Broth
steps.desc: In a separate pot, heat the broth with minced garlic and celery. Season with salt and a pinch of sugar.

steps.title: Serve
steps.desc: Place the meatballs in a bowl. Pour the hot broth over them and garnish with fried shallots and sambal if desired.

steps.title: Spread
steps.desc: Lay the tortilla flat and spread the hummus evenly across the center, leaving room at the edges.

steps.title: Layer
steps.desc: Place the cucumber slices, tomatoes, and crumbled feta on top of the hummus layer.

steps.title: Fold
steps.desc: Fold in the sides of the tortilla and roll it up tightly to enclose the filling.

steps.title: Optional Toast
steps.desc: Briefly toast the wrap in a dry pan for 1 minute per side until golden and slightly crunchy.

steps.title: Sauté Veggies
steps.desc: Heat oil and sauté garlic until fragrant. Add carrots and cabbage, stir-frying until they soften slightly.

steps.title: Scramble
steps.desc: Push the vegetables to the side of the pan. Crack the eggs into the empty space and scramble until mostly set.

steps.title: Mix
steps.desc: Toss the eggs and vegetables together. Season with salt, sugar, and white pepper.

steps.title: Finish
steps.desc: Cook for 1 more minute until the eggs are fully done and the flavors are well combined. Perfect for breakfast or a light lunch.

steps.title: Cook Noodles
steps.desc: Boil soba noodles for 4-5 minutes. Drain and rinse under cold water to stop the cooking and remove excess starch.

steps.title: Whisk Sauce
steps.desc: In a bowl, combine peanut butter, soy sauce, lime juice, chili oil, and a tablespoon of hot water. Whisk until smooth.

steps.title: Combine
steps.desc: Toss the cold noodles with the peanut sauce until every strand is thoroughly coated.

steps.title: Garnish
steps.desc: Top with crushed peanuts or sliced green onions for extra crunch and flavor.

steps.title: Initial Fry
steps.desc: Lightly fry the chicken drumsticks in oil until the skin is golden but the meat is not fully cooked. Set aside.

steps.title: Aromatics
steps.desc: Sauté the garlic, ginger, and onion in the same pan until the onion becomes translucent and fragrant.

steps.title: Simmer
steps.desc: Return the chicken to the pan. Add sweet soy sauce and 1/2 cup of water. Cover and simmer on low heat for 15 minutes.

steps.title: Glaze
steps.desc: Remove the lid and increase the heat. Cook until the liquid reduces into a thick, dark, savory syrup that coats the chicken.

steps.title: Cook Beef
steps.desc: Brown the ground beef and onion in a skillet. Stir in the taco seasoning and a splash of water, then remove from the pan.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.desc: Place one tortilla in the skillet. Sprinkle half the cheese, then the beef mixture, and top with the remaining cheese and second tortilla.

steps.title: Grill
steps.desc: Cook over medium heat for 3 minutes per side until the tortilla is golden brown and the cheese has completely melted.

steps.title: Slice
steps.desc: Slide onto a cutting board and let it rest for 1 minute before slicing into wedges. Serve with sour cream or salsa.

steps.title: Whisk Base
steps.desc: In a bowl, whisk the eggs thoroughly. Sift in the rice flour and whisk again until no lumps remain; the flour is key to the omelet's thickness.

steps.title: Add Aromatics
steps.desc: Stir in the chopped chilies, scallions, celery, salt, and a pinch of coriander powder. Ensure the vegetables are evenly distributed.

steps.title: Heat Pan
steps.desc: Heat a generous amount of oil in a small, deep skillet over medium-low heat. The oil must be hot enough to make the edges crisp immediately.

steps.title: Slow Fry
steps.desc: Pour in the egg mixture. Cook on low heat so the center sets without burning the bottom. Use a spatula to gently lift the edges.

steps.title: The Flip
steps.desc: Once the top is mostly set, carefully flip the omelet. Cook for another 3 minutes until both sides are deep golden brown and crispy.

steps.title: Sauté Garlic
steps.desc: Heat butter in a large pan over medium heat. Sauté the minced garlic for about 1 minute until fragrant.

steps.title: Simmer Gnocchi
steps.desc: Add the heavy cream and 1/4 cup of water to the pan. Bring to a gentle simmer, then add the dry gnocchi directly into the liquid.

steps.title: Cook through
steps.desc: Cover and cook for 5-7 minutes. The gnocchi will absorb the cream and become tender. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

steps.title: Wilt Greens
steps.desc: Stir in the fresh spinach and parmesan cheese. Heat until the spinach has completely wilted into the sauce.

steps.title: Season
steps.desc: Adjust seasoning with salt and plenty of cracked black pepper. Serve immediately while the sauce is velvety.

steps.title: Prepare Base
steps.desc: Boil 2 cups of water with bay leaves, galangal, and a paste made of garlic, shallots, and candlenuts (or macadamias).

steps.title: Hard Veggies
steps.desc: Add the chayote and tempeh cubes to the boiling water. Cook for 10 minutes until the chayote begins to soften.

steps.title: Coconut Milk
steps.desc: Pour in the coconut milk slowly. Keep the heat on medium and stir continuously so the coconut milk doesn't break/separate.

steps.title: Soft Veggies
steps.desc: Add the long beans and eggplant. Simmer for another 10 minutes until all vegetables are tender but not mushy.

steps.title: Final Season
steps.desc: Add salt and a teaspoon of sugar. The soup should be creamy, savory, and slightly sweet. Serve with warm rice.

steps.title: High Heat Sear
steps.desc: Heat olive oil in a skillet over high heat until smoking slightly. Pat the steak bites dry with a paper towel and season with salt.

steps.title: Brown Beef
steps.desc: Add steak bites to the pan in a single layer. Sear for 2 minutes without moving them to get a crust, then flip and sear for 1 more minute.

steps.title: Butter Bath
steps.desc: Reduce heat to medium. Add the butter, minced garlic, and parsley to the pan.

steps.title: Baste
steps.desc: Toss the steak bites in the melting garlic butter for 1-2 minutes until the garlic is golden. Remove from heat immediately to avoid overcooking.

steps.title: Rest
steps.desc: Let the meat rest in the pan for 2 minutes before serving to ensure the juices redistribute.

steps.title: Braise (Ungkep)
steps.desc: In a pot, combine chicken with garlic, coriander, salt, and enough water to half-cover the meat. Simmer until the water is almost gone.

steps.title: Make Baste
steps.desc: Mix the Kecap Manis with lime juice and a tablespoon of the remaining braising liquid or oil.

steps.title: Prep Pan
steps.desc: Heat a non-stick (Teflon) pan over medium-high heat. You don't need much oil as the chicken skin will release its own.

steps.title: Grill
steps.desc: Place the chicken on the pan. Brush generously with the soy sauce mixture. Flip every 2 minutes, reapplying the sauce each time.

steps.title: Caramelize
steps.desc: Continue grilling until the chicken has dark, charred, caramelized patches and the sauce has thickened into a sticky glaze.

steps.title: Mash Chickpeas
steps.desc: Place drained chickpeas in a bowl and mash them with a fork until they reach a flaky, tuna-like consistency.

steps.title: Mix Filling
steps.desc: Add the mayo, onion, mustard, salt, and pepper. For extra flavor, add a squeeze of lemon or chopped pickles.

steps.title: Toast Bread
steps.desc: Lightly toast your bread slices until golden brown.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.desc: Spread the chickpea mixture thickly onto one slice of bread. Top with lettuce or spinach if desired, then close the sandwich.

steps.title: Noodle Prep
steps.desc: Ensure the vermicelli is soft but still has a 'bite' (al dente). Drain thoroughly and toss with 1 tablespoon of oil to prevent clumping.

steps.title: Sauté Aromatics
steps.desc: Sauté minced garlic and white pepper in a wok until fragrant. Add the julienned carrots and shredded cabbage.

steps.title: Add Protein
steps.desc: Push veggies aside and scramble the egg in the center of the pan. Mix everything together once the egg is set.

steps.title: Combine
steps.desc: Add the noodles and sweet soy sauce. Stir-fry vigorously on high heat for 3-4 minutes so the noodles absorb the sauce evenly.

steps.title: Garnish
steps.desc: Serve with a side of pickled cucumber (acar) and a sprinkle of crispy fried shallots.

steps.title: Boil Pasta
steps.desc: Cook pasta in salted water for 2 minutes less than the package instructions. It will finish cooking in the oven.

steps.title: Brown Meat
steps.desc: In a pan, brown the ground beef. Drain excess fat and stir in the marinara sauce. Simmer for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Mix
steps.desc: In a large bowl, toss the cooked pasta with the meat sauce and the ricotta cheese until well combined.

steps.title: Layer
steps.desc: Transfer the mixture to a baking dish. Top generously with shredded mozzarella and a sprinkle of dried oregano.

steps.title: Bake
steps.desc: Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 20 minutes until the cheese is bubbly and starting to brown on top.

steps.title: Boil Noodles
steps.desc: Cook the instant noodles in boiling water until soft. Drain and set aside in a bowl.

steps.title: Mix Batter
steps.desc: Add the noodle seasoning packets, eggs, sliced spring onions, sausage, and chili to the noodles. Mix until well incorporated.

steps.title: Fry
steps.desc: Heat a tablespoon of oil in a small pan. Pour the noodle mixture in and spread it out evenly to form a circle.

steps.title: Flip
steps.desc: Cook for 4-5 minutes on medium-low heat. Once the bottom is crispy, flip carefully and cook the other side for 3 minutes.

steps.title: Serve
steps.desc: Cut into wedges like a pizza and serve with chili sauce or ketchup. A classic Indonesian student comfort food!

steps.title: Sauté Filling
steps.desc: Sauté onions and mushrooms in a pan until the mushrooms have released their moisture and are browned.

steps.title: Wilt Spinach
steps.desc: Add the spinach to the pan and cook for 1 minute until wilted. Season with salt and garlic powder, then remove from pan.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.desc: Clean the pan and place one tortilla in it. Sprinkle half the cheese, then the mushroom-spinach mix, and the rest of the cheese.

steps.title: Grill
steps.desc: Top with the second tortilla. Cook for 2-3 minutes per side over medium heat until the tortilla is crisp and the cheese is melted.

steps.title: Slice
steps.desc: Remove from heat, let it sit for 30 seconds so the cheese sets, then slice into quarters.
